title: After days and days I finally received a

messages.author: mouse ▒ (ghosts)
messages.text: Petersberg Nord is one closest to me on maps
messages.content_html: Petersberg Nord is one closest to me on maps

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: You are probably looking at needing a yagi to pull that in with any reliability.
messages.content_html: You are probably looking at needing a yagi to pull that in with any reliability.

messages.author: mouse ▒ (ghosts)
messages.text: ah damn, and that was outside on a bridge, too
messages.content_html: ah damn, and that was outside on a bridge, too

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: Space to put a yagi in your attic?
messages.content_html: Space to put a yagi in your attic?

messages.author: mouse ▒ (ghosts)
messages.text: there should be space behind that injection molded plastic "window" pointing in the right direction. I might need to move it when anyone needs to access the roof through it
messages.content_html: there should be space behind that injection molded plastic "window" pointing in the right direction. I might need to move it when anyone needs to access the roof through it

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: given how extremely low that packet game in, I really don't think you're gonna get anything without a yagi, and ideally a yagi outside on the roof (which I know you've said you don't have access to).
messages.content_html: given how extremely low that packet game in, I really don't think you're gonna get anything without a yagi, and ideally a yagi outside on the roof (which I know you've said you don't have access to).

messages.author: mouse ▒ (ghosts)
messages.text: before I dive into it, how much will I likely pay for a decent yagi
messages.content_html: before I dive into it, how much will I likely pay for a decent yagi

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: I've no idea what yagis cost. There are plenty of videos on YouTube about how to DIY one if you're so inclined.
messages.content_html: I've no idea what yagis cost. There are plenty of videos on YouTube about how to DIY one if you're so inclined.

messages.author: mouse ▒ (ghosts)
messages.text: fair. I will take a look
messages.content_html: fair. I will take a look

messages.author: mouse ▒ (ghosts)
messages.text: Thank you so much for helping me figure out how to get on the mesh!
messages.content_html: Thank you so much for helping me figure out how to get on the mesh!

messages.author: Pixelguy
messages.text: What antenna are you currently using? Also: You should print and frame your first packet.
messages.content_html: What antenna are you currently using? Also: You should print and frame your first packet.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: mouse ▒ (ghosts)
messages.text: the antenna that came with the Cardputer ADV + Cap LoRa, so a (probably relatively bad) stubby angle-able one that I used un-angled, outside, on a bridge, held high, with probable line of sight
messages.content_html: the antenna that came with the Cardputer ADV + Cap LoRa, so a (probably relatively bad) stubby angle-able one that I used un-angled, outside, on a bridge, held high, with probable line of sight

messages.author: Pixelguy
messages.text: Try this Antenna: https://www.amazon.de/dp/B0F1CVG85K Myself and others in the german community use it with great success. Using a stubby antenna my strongest RX signal is around -6 dB. With the antenna above its a solid 6/9dB. Not sure how the Cardputer antenna compares. Since it's for enthusiasts I would assume it is not completely shite. (edited)
messages.content_html: Try this Antenna: https://www.amazon.de/dp/B0F1CVG85K Myself and others in the german community use it with great success. Using a stubby antenna my strongest RX signal is around -6 dB. With the antenna above its a solid 6/9dB. Not sure how the Cardputer antenna compares. Since it's for enthusiasts I would assume it is not completely shite. (edited)
embeds_html:
868-MHz-Antenne, 10 dBi Gain für Meshtastic LoRa Long Range Soft W...
868-MHz-Antenne, 10 dBi Gain für Meshtastic LoRa Long Range Soft Whip-Antenne, 17 cm Omni SMA-Stecker, schwarz (1er-Pack)

messages.author: mouse ▒ (ghosts)
messages.text: thank you! I'll give it a shot. if that doesn't get me far enough I'll try building a yagi out of measuring tape and pvc pipes
messages.content_html: thank you! I'll give it a shot. if that doesn't get me far enough I'll try building a yagi out of measuring tape and pvc pipes

With the antenna above its a solid 6/9dB. Not sure how the Cardputer antenna compares. Since it's for enthusiasts I would assume it is not completely shite. (edited)

messages.author: mouse ▒ (ghosts)
messages.text: I definitely should have had line of sight to the hill from the bridge, I assume I have line of sight to the hill from the plastic attic window, at least the data suggests so. (edited)
messages.content_html: I definitely should have had line of sight to the hill from the bridge, I assume I have line of sight to the hill from the plastic attic window, at least the data suggests so. (edited)
messages.reply_html:
Pixelguy
I think you mentioned somewhere, that you have line of sight to that hill? If that is you. That plastic attic window rings a bell (edited)

messages.author: mouse ▒ (ghosts)
messages.text: I was on the bridge again and was able to Rx a few packets, mostly -12 dB but a couple with -8 and one with -5 and all terminating in the same e4 node. That gives me hope that I will be luckier getting a packet out with a better antenna
messages.content_html: I was on the bridge again and was able to Rx a few packets, mostly -12 dB but a couple with -8 and one with -5 and all terminating in the same e4 node. That gives me hope that I will be luckier getting a packet out with a better antenna

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: There is an export option in the receive log screen, found in the button in the upper right. You'd have to save periodicially on your own, though, if it app is unstable for you.
messages.content_html: There is an export option in the receive log screen, found in the button in the upper right. You'd have to save periodicially on your own, though, if it app is unstable for you.
messages.reply_html:
mouse ▒ (ghosts)
I wish I could look through the packets now but the meshcore app crashed and took the packet list with it

messages.author: mouse ▒ (ghosts)
messages.text: I see. thanks for letting me know
messages.content_html: I see. thanks for letting me know

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: FWIW, I don't recall the app ever crashing on me. However, the OS will sometimes kill it as part of its over aggressive battery management.
messages.content_html: FWIW, I don't recall the app ever crashing on me. However, the OS will sometimes kill it as part of its over aggressive battery management.

messages.author: mouse ▒ (ghosts)
messages.text: I was switching between the app and the letsmesh map
messages.content_html: I was switching between the app and the letsmesh map

messages.author: Pixelguy
messages.text: Do you get any repeats at all on your message when sending a short "test" nearish the repeater? try sending short messages, they have higher chance to get heard/repeated than longer messages or adverts.
messages.content_html: Do you get any repeats at all on your message when sending a short "test" nearish the repeater? try sending short messages, they have higher chance to get heard/repeated than longer messages or adverts.

messages.author: mouse ▒ (ghosts)
messages.text: none at all, even for short "test" blurbs in the public channel. I think my transmit is too weak. I don't see repeats, I don't see any repeaters using the discover nodes mode, all I can do is passively capture a few flood packets (edited)
messages.content_html: none at all, even for short "test" blurbs in the public channel. I think my transmit is too weak. I don't see repeats, I don't see any repeaters using the discover nodes mode, all I can do is passively capture a few flood packets (edited)
messages.reply_html:
Pixelguy
Do you get any repeats at all on your message when sending a short "test" nearish the repeater? try sending short messages, they have higher chance to get heard/repeated than longer messages or adverts.

messages.author: mouse ▒ (ghosts)
messages.text: like a mad person I keep going out to the bridge, still with the same antenna, expecting the same actions to yield different results. they did! this time despite no node hearing me still, there were two adverts I could hear, and they got auto added as my first contacts
messages.content_html: like a mad person I keep going out to the bridge, still with the same antenna, expecting the same actions to yield different results. they did! this time despite no node hearing me still, there were two adverts I could hear, and they got auto added as my first contacts
messages.reactions_html: 1
